text:
Vahagn Nersesyan
Vahagn Nersesyan is an Associate Professor of Mathematics at NYU Shanghai and an Associated Professor at the Courant Institute of Mathematics at NYU. He obtained his PhD from the University of Paris-Sud and his Habilitation from the University of Paris-Saclay (Versailles campus). His main research interests are in Probability Theory and Partial Differential Equations.
Select Publications
V. Nersesyan, M. Rissel, Localized and degenerate controls for the incompressible Navier–Stokes system, Comm. Pure Appl. Math., 78(7), 1285-1319, 2025.
A. Duca, V. Nersesyan, Bilinear control and growth of Sobolev norms for the nonlinear Schrödinger equation, J. Eur. Math. Soc., 27(6), 2603-2622, 2025.
V. Nersesyan, M. Rissel, Global controllability of Boussinesq flows by using only a temperature control, Arch. Ration. Mech. Anal., 249(5), Paper No. 58, 2025.
V. Nersesyan, D. Zhang, C. Zhou, On the chaotic behavior of the Lagrangian flow of the 2D Navier–Stokes system with bounded degenerate noise, arXiv:2406.17612, 2024.
V. Nersesyan, M. Zhao, Polynomial mixing for the white-forced Navier-Stokes system in the whole space, arXiv:2410.15727, 2024.
